workspace.title: WorkSpace
workspace.coreFilesDesc: Bootstrap persona, identity, and tool guidance.
workspace.uploadTooltip: Bootstrap persona, identity, and tool guidance. (ZIP files only, max 100MB)
workspace.zipOnly: Only .zip files are supported for upload
workspace.fileSizeExceeded: File size exceeds 100MB limit. Current file: {{size}}MB
workspace.attribution: Workspace design partly inspired by the OpenClaw project — thank you! 🐾

skills.title: Skills
skills.invalidSkillUrlSource: Skill URL currently needs to start with https://skills.sh/, https://clawhub.ai/, https://skillsmp.com/, or https://github.com/
skills.contentPlaceholder:
---
name: <skill_name> (required)
description: <skill description> (required)
metadata: { "copaw": { "emoji": "🔧" } }
---

Skill implementation content...

# Example:
# ---
# name: cron
# description: Manage cron jobs via copaw commands - create, query, pause, resume, delete tasks
# metadata: { "copaw": { "emoji": "⏰" } }
# ---
skills.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this skill?
skills.frontmatterRequired: Skills must start and end with ---
skills.editNotSupported: Edit operation is not supported by backend API
skills.editNote: Note: Backend API does not support editing skills. You can only view or toggle enable/disable status.

mcp.title: MCP Clients
mcp.toggleError: Failed to toggle MCP client status
mcp.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this MCP client?
mcp.name: Name
mcp.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of what this MCP server provides
mcp.commandRequired: Command is required for stdio transport
mcp.urlRequired: URL is required for SSE / Streamable HTTP transport
mcp.headersHelp: JSON object of HTTP headers (e.g., auth tokens)

heartbeat.title: Heartbeat
heartbeat.description: Run HEARTBEAT.md at a fixed interval for self-checks. By default runs silently without affecting current conversations, or optionally send replies to the last chat channel.

cronJobs.title: Cron Jobs
cronJobs.description: Create and manage scheduled tasks that automatically execute at specified times.
cronJobs.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this Cron Job?
cronJobs.name: Job Name
cronJobs.taskDescriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of what this job does...
cronJobs.idTooltip: Unique identifier for this job. Use lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores.
cronJobs.nameTooltip: A friendly name to help you identify this job.
cronJobs.cronTooltip: Define when the task should run
cronJobs.cronExample: Common examples: '0 9 * * *' = 9 AM daily | '*/30 * * * *' = every 30 min | '0 */2 * * *' = every 2 hours | '0 0 * * 0' = Sunday midnight
cronJobs.timezoneTooltip: Timezone for the cron schedule. Default: UTC
cronJobs.taskTypeTooltip: Choose 'text' for simple message tasks, or 'agent' for complex agent workflows.
cronJobs.textTooltip: Optional description of what this task does. Helpful for documentation.
cronJobs.requestInputTooltip: Message content in JSON format. This is what the agent will receive and process.
cronJobs.requestSessionIdTooltip: Session ID for the request context. Use 'default' if unsure.
cronJobs.requestUserIdTooltip: User ID that initiates the request. Use 'system' for automated tasks.
cronJobs.dispatchChannelTooltip: Target channel where the response will be sent (e.g., 'console', 'discord', 'imessage').
cronJobs.dispatchTargetUserIdTooltip: User ID who will receive the response in the target channel.
cronJobs.dispatchTargetSessionIdTooltip: Session ID where the response will be delivered in the target channel.
cronJobs.dispatchModeTooltip: Choose 'stream' for real-time responses or 'final' for complete responses only.
cronJobs.maxConcurrencyTooltip: Maximum number of this job that can run simultaneously. Default: 1
cronJobs.timeoutSecondsTooltip: Maximum execution time in seconds. Job will be terminated if exceeded.
cronJobs.misfireGraceSecondsTooltip: Grace period for missed executions. If a job misses its scheduled time by more than this, it won't run.
cronJobs.executeNowContent: Are you sure you want to execute "{{name}}" now?

channels.title: Channels
channels.filterToolMessagesTooltip: Show tool call and output messages to users (turn off to hide)
channels.filterThinkingTooltip: Show model thinking/reasoning content to users (turn off to hide)
channels.showToolDetailsDesc: When enabled, tool call arguments and outputs are rendered in channel replies.
channels.showToolDetailsSaveFailed: Failed to save tool detail display setting
channels.showToolDetailsLoadFailed: Failed to load tool detail display setting
channels.dmPolicyTooltip: Control who can interact with the bot via direct message. Open: everyone; Allowlist: authorized users only
channels.groupPolicyTooltip: Control who can interact with the bot in group chats. Open: everyone; Allowlist: authorized users only
channels.allowFromTooltip: List of user IDs allowed to interact with the bot (format: nickname#last4, e.g. John#1234)
channels.allowFromPlaceholder: Enter user ID and press Enter to add
channels.phoneNumberSidHelp: Found under Phone Numbers → Active Numbers in the Twilio Console.
channels.voiceSetupGuide: Set up a Twilio account, purchase a phone number, then enter your credentials below. Find your Account SID and Auth Token on the Twilio Console dashboard. The Phone Number SID is listed under Phone Numbers → Active Numbers.
channels.showReasoningDesc: When enabled, model REASONING/thinking content is rendered in channel replies.
channels.showReasoningSaveFailed: Failed to save reasoning display setting
channels.renderVisibilityLoadFailed: Failed to load channel render visibility settings

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.description: View and manage active chat sessions
sessions.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this session?
sessions.batchDeleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete selected {{count}} sessions?

environments.title: Environment Variables
environments.description: Configure key-value environment variables for agents and skills.

models.providersDescription: Configure API keys and endpoints for each provider.
models.llmDescription: Choose the active LLM model from an authorized provider.
models.protocolHint: Choose the provider API protocol for this configuration.
models.leaveBlankKeep: Leave blank to keep current key
models.openAICompatibleEndpoint: OpenAI-compatible endpoint, e.g. https://api.example.com (append /v1 only if your provider requires it)
models.anthropicEndpointHint: Anthropic endpoint, e.g. https://api.anthropic.com (append /v1 if your deployment requires it)
models.pleaseEnterBaseURL: Please enter the API base URL
models.revokeConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to remove the API key for {{name}}? The current LLM model configuration will also be cleared.
models.revokeConfirmSimple: Are you sure you want to remove the API key for {{name}}?
models.providerIdHint: Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, underscores. Cannot be changed later.
models.deleteProviderConfirm: Delete custom provider "{{name}}" and all its models? This cannot be undone.
models.localFilenameHint: Leave empty to auto-select the best quantization
models.localDeleteConfirm: Delete local model "{{name}}"? The model file will be removed from disk.
models.localDownloading: Downloading {{repo}}... This may take a few minutes.
models.localDownloadNavigateHint: You can navigate away — the download will continue in the background.
models.localDownloadInProgress: A download is already in progress
models.testConnectionError: An error occurred while testing connection
models.modelTestFailed: Model validation failed, please check if the model ID is correct
models.modelTestFailedConfirm: Model connection test failed: {{message}}. Do you still want to add this model?
models.autoDiscoveredAndAdded: Auto-discovered {{count}} models and added {{added}} new model(s)
models.autoDiscoveredNoNew: Auto-discovered {{count}} models; model list is already up to date
models.autoDiscoverFailed: Automatic model discovery failed; you can add models manually

agentConfig.title: Configuration
agentConfig.maxItersTooltip: Maximum number of reasoning-acting iterations for ReAct agent
agentConfig.maxItersMin: Max iterations must be at least 1
agentConfig.maxInputLengthTooltip: Maximum input length (tokens) for the model context window
agentConfig.maxInputLengthMin: Max input length must be at least 1000

botProfiles.title: Bot Profiles
botProfiles.description: Configure one bot profile per agent persona with independent memory namespace.
botProfiles.notice: Session and memory are isolated by bot profile. Channel bindings route inbound channel traffic to the selected bot profile.
botProfiles.loadFailed: Failed to load bot profiles configuration
botProfiles.saveFailed: Failed to save bot profiles configuration
botProfiles.defaultBotRequired: Please select a default bot profile
botProfiles.atLeastOneProfile: At least one bot profile is required

subagents.title: Subagents
subagents.description: Configure subagent orchestration and monitor task execution.
subagents.maxConcurrencyMin: Max concurrency must be at least 1
subagents.defaultTimeoutSecondsMin: Default timeout must be at least 1 second
subagents.hardTimeoutSecondsMin: Hard timeout must be at least 1 second
subagents.timeoutRelationInvalid: Hard timeout must be greater than or equal to default timeout
subagents.directModeWarning: Direct mode writes into target paths directly. Please verify allowed paths and permissions carefully.
subagents.allowNestedSpawnTooltip: Allow subagents to spawn child subagents.
subagents.autoDispatchKeywordsTooltip: Keyword-based force routing in force_when_matched mode.
subagents.autoDispatchMinPromptCharsMin: Minimum prompt length must be at least 1
subagents.defaultRoleTooltip: Fallback role when keyword routing misses.
subagents.defaultRoleNeedsRoles: No roles configured yet. Add at least one role in Roles tab, then choose a default role.
subagents.defaultRoleDisabledPlaceholder: Switch role selection mode to Always Default Role
subagents.allowedPathsTooltip: Paths that subagents can access in direct mode.
subagents.defaultToolsTooltip: Tool IDs enabled for subagents by default.
subagents.mcpSelectedTooltip: Active when MCP policy is set to selected.
subagents.skillsSelectedTooltip: Active when skill policy is set to selected.
subagents.allPolicyWarning: Policy set to all increases capability and risk. Use only in trusted environments.
subagents.roleModelPolicyHint: Model policy lets this role pick a specific provider/model pair and fallback chain.
subagents.rolesHint: Define specialist roles with identity and tool guidance. Main agent routes tasks by policy.
subagents.label: Label

modelConfig.promptMessage: Chat requires an LLM model to function. Without a configured model, conversations and messages cannot be sent to the backend for processing and storage, and will be lost after a page refresh. Would you like to configure a model now?
modelConfig.chatDisabledMessage: Chat functionality is disabled because no model is configured. Please configure a model to enable chat.
modelConfig.modelNotConfigured: Please configure a model in Settings before using chat

sidebar.newVersion: New version available: v{{version}}, click to upgrade
